common.tagline: A safe dialogue with your mind.
common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.headline: Talk freely. Heal gently. Grow stronger.
hero.subheading: Questionnaires, AI chat, and personal support — all in one place. Not judgment — listening.

step1.title: Answer a few questions
step1.description: Age-appropriate questions help you notice how you've been feeling — not a test, just supportive screening.

step2.title: Chat with AI companion
step2.description: Talk freely with Mon-Songlap — a warm, non-judgmental AI that listens in Bangla or English.

step3.title: Personal support plan
step3.description: Based on your answers — small daily steps, mood tracking, and referral guidance when you need professional support.

voice.title: What your voice carries matters too
voice.description: Sometimes writing is hard. Speak into the mic — we'll offer supportive insight from tone and pace, not a clinical verdict.

privacy.title: Your privacy, stated clearly
privacy.statement1: Your personal data is never sold or shared with schools, family, or employers.
privacy.statement2: Your conversations and journal are encrypted; you can delete them anytime.
privacy.statement3: We do not sell your data. We do not use it for advertising.
privacy.statement4: If you're under 18, guardian consent is required — but they cannot see your personal conversations.

quote1.text: I was embarrassed to tell anyone about exam stress. Here I can write without being judged — just heard.

quote2.text: After screening I realized I wasn't alone. The referral card gave me courage to see a counselor.

quote3.text: Being able to speak in Bangla is huge. I can't express my feelings properly in English apps.

q1.question: Will my information be shared with anyone?
q1.answer: No. Your conversations and journal are yours alone — not sent to school, family, or friends. No one can see them except in an emergency situation.

q2.question: Is this a replacement for a doctor?
q2.answer: No, not at all. Mon-Songlap offers screening and supportive insight — not treatment or diagnosis. We'll guide you toward professional help when needed.

q3.question: If I seek mental health support, will everyone know?
q3.answer: You can keep your identity private here. Seeking support for your wellbeing is not weakness — it's self-care.

q4.question: Is it safe?
q4.answer: Yes. Your data is encrypted and can be deleted anytime. Bangladesh helplines are always visible for crisis support.

q5.question: Why is it free?
q5.answer: Our goal is to make mental health support accessible for students. All core features will remain free.

footer.tagline: A safe dialogue with your mind.

guardianConsent.title: Guardian consent
guardianConsent.subtitle: You're under 18 — guardian consent is required to activate your account
guardianConsent.explanation: Your guardian will know you're using this service — but they cannot see your personal conversations, journal, or screening results.
guardianConsent.consentCheckbox: I consent to my child using Mon-Songlap

errors.weakPassword: Password must be at least 8 characters

age.title: How old are you?
age.subtitle: We'll show age-appropriate screening questions
age.note: Your answer is only used for screening — it won't be shared with anyone

consent.title: Consent & privacy
consent.subtitle: Please read before you begin
consent.dataUse: Your information is used only to support you
consent.notDiagnosis: This is not treatment or diagnosis — screening and supportive insight
consent.deleteAnytime: You can delete your data anytime
consent.emergency: In a crisis, safety resources may be shown for your protection

language.title: Language & accessibility
language.subtitle: Set your preferences — you can change them later

chat.welcome: Hi 👋 I'm Mon-Songlap. I'm here to listen without judgment. How is your mind today?

suggestions.chip3: I want to ease exam stress
suggestions.chip4: I just want to talk to someone

safetyCard.title: You're not alone
safetyCard.message: If you'd like to talk to someone right now, you can reach these numbers. They're free and confidential.

journal.title: Mood journal
journal.subtitle: How is your mind today? Jot it down briefly
journal.notePlaceholder: What happened today, how you're feeling…

calendar.title: Mood calendar
calendar.subtitle: A picture of your mind, day by day

sleep.title: Sleep
sleep.label: How many hours did you sleep last night?

hydration.title: Hydration
hydration.label: How much water did you drink today?

exercise.title: Exercise
exercise.label: Did you walk or exercise today?

meditation.title: Mindfulness
meditation.label: Did you take a moment to be still today?

gratitude.title: Gratitude
gratitude.label: What are you grateful for today?

reflection.title: Reflection
reflection.label: Reflect on your day a little
reflection.placeholder: What you learned, what felt good…

breathing.title: Breathing
breathing.label: Did you do a breathing exercise today?

habitTracker.title: Habit tracker
habitTracker.subtitle: Small steps, meaningful change
habitTracker.streakBreak: You missed a day — that's okay, start again

riskIndicators.title: Mind indicators
riskIndicators.explanation: These are screening-based observations — not treatment. If you're concerned, speak with a professional.

assessment.title: Smart screening
assessment.subtitle: Take a moment — we'll help you understand your mind
assessment.intro: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following?
assessment.optionalNote: You can write more if you'd like (optional)

phq9.title: PHQ-9 screening
phq9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9.q6: Feeling bad about yourself — or like a failure
phq9.q8: Moving or speaking slowly, or being restless or fidgety
phq9.q9: Thoughts of hurting yourself or that you'd be better off dead

gad7.title: GAD-7 screening
gad7.q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
gad7.q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
gad7.q3: Worrying too much about different things
gad7.q5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
gad7.q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

phqa.title: PHQ-A screening (youth)
phqa.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phqa.q3: Trouble falling asleep or sleeping too much
phqa.q4: Feeling tired or low on energy

rcads.title: RCADS screening
rcads.q3: I'm afraid something bad will happen
rcads.q4: I feel embarrassed talking at school or with friends

results.title: Your screening result
results.disclaimer: This is a screening result — not treatment or diagnosis. Speaking with a professional can give you clearer insight.

minimal.title: Minimal
minimal.description: Your answers suggest little stress or low mood right now. Keep up regular mood check-ins.

mild.title: Mild
mild.description: Some worry or tiredness is showing up. Small self-care steps and conversation may help.

moderate.title: Moderate
moderate.description: Your answers show some concerning signs. Speaking with a counselor or mental health professional would be a good step.

high.title: High
high.description: Your answers suggest serious concern. Please talk to someone today — use the helplines below or a trusted person.

summary.minimal: From your answers, it seems you're doing relatively well. Writing in your mood journal now and then can help you understand yourself better.
summary.mild: Some days have been hard — that's normal. You're not alone. Small steps and talking to someone can make things easier.
summary.moderate: Your mind needs understanding right now. Speaking with a professional makes it easier to find the right support — that's not weakness.
summary.high: From what you've shared, you seem to be in a lot of pain. Please reach out to someone today — you're not alone, help is available.

referral.title: Professional support suggestion
referral.message: Based on your screening result, speaking with a mental health professional would be a good step.

voice.title: Voice check-in
voice.subtitle: Speak — get a gentle sense of how you're feeling

consentModal.title: Recording consent
consentModal.description: Your voice will be used only for analysis in this session. It won't be shared with third parties. You can delete it anytime.
consentModal.notStored: Recordings are not permanently stored on the server

analysis.title: Analysis result
analysis.explanation: Speaking quickly can sometimes signal anxiety — but context matters too. It's not the only truth.

voice.disclaimer: This analysis offers supportive insight — not a clinical opinion or diagnosis.

mindGym.title: Mind Gym
mindGym.subtitle: Practice mental skills in a safe space
mindGym.reflectionPlaceholder: What did you learn from this scenario? How did you feel?

recovery.title: 30-day support plan
recovery.subtitle: Small steps, at your own pace

tips.minimal: You're doing well. Small regular habits keep the mind steady.
tips.mild: Rest a little. You did something good today — write it down.
tips.moderate: Don't push yourself today. One small task is enough — a walk or breathing exercise.
tips.high: Your most important task today is self-care. Talk to someone if you need to.

insights.title: AI insights
insights.sleep: Sleep was low last week — try going to bed a little earlier tonight.
insights.exercise: Mood was better on walking days. How about 15 minutes outside today?
insights.gratitude: Gratitude writing helped your mood — try one small thing today too.

emergency.title: Crisis support
emergency.subtitle: You're not alone — you can talk to someone right now

safetyPlan.title: Safety plan
safetyPlan.subtitle: What to do in hard moments — write it down beforehand

profile.title: Profile
profile.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? All data will be deleted — this cannot be undone.

dataPrivacy.title: Data & privacy
dataPrivacy.deleteWarning: This is permanent — all conversations, journals, and reports will be deleted

about.title: About us
about.description: Mon-Songlap is an AI-supported mental health companion for Bangladeshi students.

weekly.summary: This week you checked in 5 times — average mood 5.8. Mood was better on days you walked and wrote gratitude.

monthly.title: Monthly report
monthly.summary: Mood was steady this month. Screening score dropped 2 points — small steps are working.
